product.name: Aramid Vis 150
product.categorySourceGroup: Inherent Flame Retardant Aramid Viscose Fr Woven Workwear Fabrics
product.titleDescriptor: Aramid Viscose Fr Fabric, 53% Viscose Fr 40% meta Aramid 5% para Aramid 2% Carbon , 150 gr/m'2
product.specLine: 150 g/m², 53% Viscose fr 40% m-Aramid 5% p-Aramid 2% Carbon, Inherent Flame Retardant, Antistatic, 1/1 Plain Fabric Aramid Viscose fr blend fabric
descriptions: 150 g/m², 53% viscose fr 40% m-Aramid, 5% p-Aramid, 2% Carbon.
for firefighters cloth inside lining, , oil and gas industry workers.
With high contect of viscose fr fiber, the fabric is ultra comfortable to wear and and absorb sweat. Inherent flame retardant fabric with EN ISO 11612 certification.
Resistant to most industrial chemicals. (EN ISO 13034)
Aramid Viscose Fr Fabric, 53% Viscose Fr 40% meta Aramid 5% para Aramid 2% Carbon , 150 gr/m'2
150 g/m², 53% Viscose fr 40% m-Aramid 5% p-Aramid 2% Carbon, Inherent Flame Retardant, Antistatic, 1/1 Plain Fabric Aramid Viscose fr blend fabric
technicalParameters: Fiber | 53% Viscose Fr (cv fr) 40% m-Aramid (m-AR) 5% p-Aramid (p-AR) 2% Carbon(CF) | EN ISO 2076
Tensile Strength | Warp > 750N; Weft > 600N | EN ISO 13934-1
Tear Strength | Warp>40N ; Weft>30N | EN ISO 13937-2
Dimensional Change | -3% < Length < +3% ; -3% < Width < +3% | EN ISO 5077
Dry Cleaning Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 D01
Hot Press Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 X11
Perspiration Light Fast. | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 B07
Azo Test | No Azo Colorants. | EN ISO 14362-1
Flame and Heat Protection | Yes | EN ISO 11612

possibleButNotAccepted.reason: Explicit mention of oil and gas industry workers as intended users places the fabric directly in the energy sector.
Explicit statement that the fabric is used as firefighter cloth inside lining, directly supporting firefighting application.
The product explicitly lists hi-vis as a property and is certified to EN ISO 20471, the high-visibility safety standard, directly supporting hi-vis application.
Technical parameter lists 'Application | Pilot Costumes' which could relate to military aviation, but no explicit military, police or uniformed-service wording is given.
'Pilot Costumes' could hint at aviation transport use, but there is no explicit statement tying the fabric to vehicles, aircraft or vessels.
EN ISO 11611 is the explicit standard for protective clothing used in welding and allied processes, directly tying the fabric to welding/metal-splash protection.

evidence: for firefighters cloth inside lining, oil and gas industry workers.
Firefighter lining viscose fr (application image label)
Inherent flame retardant fabric with EN ISO 11612 certification
primary.rationale: The product description explicitly states use as firefighter cloth inside lining, reinforced by the application image label 'Firefighter lining viscose fr' and EN ISO 11612 heat/flame certification, making firefighting the most direct and explicitly stated end use.

evidence: for firefighters cloth inside lining, , oil and gas industry workers.
Resistant to most industrial chemicals (EN ISO 13034)
secondary.rationale: Oil and gas industry workers are explicitly named as an intended user group, independently supporting the energy sector application alongside chemical resistance and antistatic certification relevant to that environment.

alternativesConsidered.reasonNotSelected: EN ISO 11611 certification is present but no explicit welding, foundry or molten-metal use case is stated beyond the standard itself.
Hi-vis is listed as a property with EN ISO 20471 certification, but no explicit high-visibility garment end-use description is given; secondary slot already filled by an independently stated use.
Pilot Costumes parameter is ambiguous and does not explicitly state military, police or uniformed-service use.
No explicit vehicle, aircraft or vessel use is stated; 'Pilot Costumes' is too ambiguous to support transport.
More specific explicit uses (firefighting, energy) dominate over generic industrial workwear framing.

modelOutput.reviewReason: Multiple explicit hazard-related uses (firefighting lining, oil and gas workers, welding standard, hi-vis certification) are present in the same source, requiring judgment on which are genuine end uses versus supporting standards.
